description_long: This drone features a striking design inspired by a wasp or bee, with a segmented body and bold black and orange striping. Its two large, circular rotors are mounted on articulated arms above the main body, providing lift and stability. The head section is angular and mechanical, with antenna-like protrusions and what appears to be a sensor or camera array at the front. The legs are thin, jointed, and metallic, resembling insect legs, and are positioned for stable landings. The tail section is tapered and segmented, echoing the abdomen of a wasp, and the overall construction suggests a lightweight yet durable build. The drone's exterior is primarily composed of metal and plastic, with some transparent or mesh elements visible in the rotor housings.
description: A wasp-inspired drone with two large circular rotors, black and orange striping, and articulated insect-like legs. Its angular head features antennae and a possible camera array.
description_view_0: Front view shows the angular head with antennae, the two large rotors above, and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the segmented body, tapered tail, and the articulated legs, as well as the profile of the rotors and head.
description_view_2: Rear view emphasizes the striped, tapered tail and the symmetrical placement of the rotors and legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the other profile of the head, body, and legs, with clear visibility of the rotor structure.
